mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and financial stress, which lifts equity valuations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when the rate cut is part of an easing cycle rather than a one-off move.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with declining FEDFUNDS do not produce higher 20-day SPX returns than flat or rising FEDFUNDS periods, or if the sign reverses in more than half of 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above the recent trend, real rates become less supportive and the market prices tighter policy, which reduces S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is driven by surprise inflation acceleration, not the level alone.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.3 percentage points above its 6-month year-over-year trend
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-acceleration episodes are followed by non-negative 20-day SPX returns in most cases, or if the negative return differential disappears once FEDFUNDS is already falling.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and demand, which should depress corporate revenue expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should be clearest when unemployment is rising from a low base rather than during already-high unemployment.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not materially lower after UNRATE rise triggers, or if PAYEMS growth offsets the signal and the combined labor deterioration still fails to predict losses.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS indicates improving nominal consumption and revenue conditions, which supports earnings expectations and raises S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism requires broad demand improvement rather than a single-month spike.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth stays positive for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if sustained positive RSAFS growth does not precede above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect vanishes when GDP growth is already decelerating.

mechanism_narrative: An upswing in INDPRO together with stronger HOUST signals real-economy production and construction momentum, which improves cyclical earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is interaction-driven because production strength without housing strength is weaker.
failure_condition: Reject if joint INDPRO and HOUST strength does not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth slows sharply while UNRATE rises, labor-market deterioration signals weakening demand and margin pressure, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. This short-horizon signal should fire only when both employment creation weakens and unemployment worsens.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth turns negative and UNRATE rises
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous PAYEMS weakness and UNRATE rises do not produce negative 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the joint condition is no better than either input alone.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST after a prior housing expansion signals cooling demand and tighter cyclical conditions, which should weigh on equities over the next 60 trading days. The bearish effect should be strongest when housing starts fall from above-trend levels.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if housing-start declines fail to precede weaker 60-day SPX returns, or if drawdowns in HOUST are followed by positive equity performance in a majority of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising INDPRO with stable or falling UNRATE indicates supply normalization and operating leverage gains, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism fails when industrial output rises because of inventory restocking but labor conditions keep worsening.
failure_condition: Reject if rising INDPRO with non-increasing UNRATE does not predict higher 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal disappears when PAYEMS growth is negative for the same window.
